description_long: This vase features a tall, narrow neck that flares slightly at the rim, transitioning into a bulbous lower body with pronounced, organic surface textures. The glaze is predominantly a gradient of pale cream to blue-green, with areas of pooling and drips that create a dynamic, almost aquatic effect. The surface is glossy and uneven, with raised, knotted forms that give the vase a sculptural, hand-crafted appearance. The base is flat and sits atop a simple, square plinth, suggesting it is intended for display. Some sides show more pronounced textural buildup and color pooling, while others are smoother and lighter. The overall impression is of a contemporary, artistic piece that combines traditional vessel form with expressive, modern glazing techniques.
description: A tall, narrow-necked vase with a bulbous, textured body and a glossy blue-green and cream glaze, set on a square plinth. The surface features organic, sculptural forms and dynamic color transitions.
description_view_0: Front view shows the vase's tall neck, bulbous body, and the most even gradient from cream to blue-green, with pronounced surface textures.
description_view_1: This side reveals more dramatic textural buildup and darker blue-green pooling, with a visible ridge and more shadowed areas.
description_view_2: The back view highlights the deepest color pooling and the most irregular, knotted surface forms, with the plinth's edges more visible.
description_view_3: This angle shows a smoother, lighter side of the vase, with less pronounced textural buildup and a more subtle color transition.
